title: Recreational Vehicle Service Technicians
description: Diagnose, inspect, adjust, repair, or overhaul recreational vehicles including travel trailers. May specialize in maintaining gas, electrical, hydraulic, plumbing, or chassis/towing systems as well as repairing generators, appliances, and interior components. Includes workers who perform customized van conversions.

tasks.text: Diagnose and repair furnace or air conditioning systems.
tasks.rationale: Complex problem-solving, strategic leadership, or high-empathy task requiring human judgment.

tasks.text: Connect electrical systems to outside power sources, and activate switches to test the operation of appliances or light fixtures.
tasks.rationale: Semi-structured task combining routine and non-routine responsibilities.

tasks.text: Examine or test operation of parts or systems to ensure completeness of repairs.
tasks.rationale: Semi-structured task combining routine and non-routine responsibilities.

tasks.text: Repair plumbing or propane gas lines, using caulking compounds and plastic or copper pipe.
tasks.rationale: Semi-structured task combining routine and non-routine responsibilities.

tasks.text: Inspect recreational vehicles to diagnose problems and perform necessary adjustment, repair, or overhaul.
tasks.rationale: Complex problem-solving, strategic leadership, or high-empathy task requiring human judgment.

tasks.text: Locate and repair frayed wiring, broken connections, or incorrect wiring, using ohmmeters, soldering irons, tape, or hand tools.
tasks.rationale: Semi-structured task combining routine and non-routine responsibilities.

tasks.text: Confer with customers, read work orders, or examine vehicles needing repair to determine the nature and extent of damage.
tasks.rationale: Complex problem-solving, strategic leadership, or high-empathy task requiring human judgment.

tasks.text: Repair leaks with caulking compound or replace pipes, using pipe wrenches.
tasks.rationale: Semi-structured task combining routine and non-routine responsibilities.

tasks.text: Inspect, repair, or replace brake systems.
tasks.rationale: Semi-structured cognitive task involving analysis, inspection, or monitoring.

tasks.text: Connect water hoses to inlet pipes of plumbing systems, and test operation of toilets or sinks.
tasks.rationale: Semi-structured task combining routine and non-routine responsibilities.

tasks.text: List parts needed, estimate costs, and plan work procedures, using parts lists, technical manuals, or diagrams.
tasks.rationale: Complex problem-solving, strategic leadership, or high-empathy task requiring human judgment.

tasks.text: Remove damaged exterior panels, and repair and replace structural frame members.
tasks.rationale: Semi-structured task combining routine and non-routine responsibilities.

tasks.text: Reset hardware, using chisels, mallets, and screwdrivers.
tasks.rationale: Semi-structured task combining routine and non-routine responsibilities.

tasks.text: Open and close doors, windows, or drawers to test their operation, trimming edges to fit, as necessary.
tasks.rationale: Semi-structured task combining routine and non-routine responsibilities.

tasks.text: Explain proper operation of vehicle systems to customers.
tasks.rationale: Semi-structured task combining routine and non-routine responsibilities.

tasks.text: Refinish wood surfaces on cabinets, doors, moldings, or floors, using power sanders, putty, spray equipment, brushes, paints, or varnishes.
tasks.rationale: Complex problem-solving, strategic leadership, or high-empathy task requiring human judgment.

tasks.text: Seal open sides of modular units to prepare them for shipment, using polyethylene sheets, nails, and hammers.
tasks.rationale: Semi-structured task combining routine and non-routine responsibilities.
